title: Korean Beef Bibimbap
description: HelloFresh's Hall of Fame Korean classic — fluffy rice topped with juicy seasoned ground beef, crisp sautéed vegetables, quick-pickled scallions, and a drizzle of gochujang sauce. A beautiful bowl packed with bold flavors.
instructions: Cook rice according to package directions. Fluff with a fork and keep warm.
Make the quick pickle: thinly slice scallion whites and half the cucumber. Toss with 1 tbsp rice vinegar and a pinch of salt. Set aside for at least 10 minutes.
Make the gochujang sauce: whisk together gochujang, remaining 1 tbsp rice vinegar, honey, 1 tbsp soy sauce, and 1 tbsp sesame oil. Set aside.
Julienne or grate the carrots. Slice remaining cucumber into half-moons. Mince garlic and ginger.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground beef and cook, breaking it up, for 5–6 minutes until browned.
Add garlic, ginger, remaining 2 tbsp soy sauce, and 1 tbsp sesame oil to the beef. Cook 2 more minutes. Remove from pan.
In the same pan, sauté carrots for 2 minutes until slightly softened. Add spinach and cook 1 minute until wilted. Season with a pinch of salt.
Divide rice into bowls. Arrange beef, sautéed carrots and spinach, fresh cucumber, and pickled scallions on top.
Drizzle gochujang sauce over each bowl. Sprinkle with sesame seeds and scallion greens. Serve immediately.
tips: Gochujang can be found in the Asian foods aisle or at any Asian grocery store. Sriracha can substitute in a pinch.
For a traditional touch, top each bowl with a fried egg — the runny yolk creates a rich sauce when mixed in.
Prep all vegetables before you start cooking since everything comes together quickly.
Leftover beef keeps well in the fridge for 3 days and makes great rice bowls for lunch.
